title: HiveOptimizerIntent
description: Parsed intent from a natural language command for the Hive Global Optimizer.

sql_mutations.description: Ordered list of executable SQL statements to apply to input tables before running the optimizer. Always present — use [] if no changes are needed. Each string is a complete, ready-to-execute SQL statement using '{run_id}' as the placeholder for the actual RunID. Only UPDATE, INSERT, and DELETE are allowed (no SELECT). Every statement targeting a run-scoped table must include WHERE RunID = '{run_id}'.

sql_mutation_reasoning.description: Explanation of why these sql_mutations were generated (or why sql_mutations is empty). Always present. Covers which table mapping was used, why those column/value/WHERE conditions were chosen, and any value conversions applied. When sql_mutations is [], explain why no table changes are needed.

period_resolution_required.description: True when a year-level date (YYYY) was given and the agent cannot resolve it to a PeriodID without a clarifying question. False when the period was given as YYYY-MM-DD (exact week) or YYYY-MM (month — defaults to first week of that month).

period_label.description: The unresolved period label when period_resolution_required is true. YYYY for year only. null when period_resolution_required is false.

confidence.description: 0–1 confidence in the parsed intent. Below 0.80 must be accompanied by non-empty ambiguities. Each unresolved required field costs –0.10; each assumption (unit conversion, defaulted value) costs –0.05.

ambiguities.description: List of unresolved questions, assumptions, or unit conversions. Empty array only when confidence >= 0.80 and no clarification is needed.

plain_english.description: One to two sentence human-readable summary of what this intent will do. Always present.

examples.sql_mutation_reasoning: No table mutations required — the user only specified run configuration parameters (duration and MIP gap), which are captured entirely in run_params. No input table data needs to change.
ambiguities: Duration '2 years' converted to 104 weekly periods (52 × 2). Confirm if correct.
objective_function not specified; defaulted to 'Maximize Bank Balance'.
examples.plain_english: Run the optimizer from period 461 for 104 periods (~2 years) with a 0.5% MIP gap.

sql_mutations:
UPDATE input_investor_capital
SET InterestRate = 0.015
WHERE RunID = '{run_id}'
  AND InvestorCapitalID = 'Loan1'
examples.sql_mutation_reasoning: User said 'change interest rate on Loan1 to 1.5%' → mapped to input_investor_capital.InterestRate per the loan/interest-rate table mapping. InvestorCapitalID = 'Loan1' comes directly from the user. Value 1.5% converted to decimal 0.015.
ambiguities: RunID not specified — update will apply to whichever run context is active. Confirm target run.
examples.plain_english: Update the interest rate for Loan1 to 1.5% in input_investor_capital, then run the optimizer.

sql_mutations:
UPDATE input_cogs
SET CogsRate = 0.25
WHERE RunID = '{run_id}'
  AND CustomerGroupID = 'C1'
  AND PeriodID = 85
examples.sql_mutation_reasoning: User said 'underwriting COGS rate for C1' → mapped to input_cogs.CogsRate per the COGS table mapping. CustomerGroupID = 'C1' comes directly from the user. PeriodID = 85 was specified explicitly. CogsTypeID not specified — defaulted to the most likely underwriting type, flagged in ambiguities.
ambiguities: input_cogs has multiple COGS types (longitudinal and cross-sectional). Clarify which CogsTypeID applies to underwriting COGS.
RunID not specified — confirm which run's input data to update.
examples.plain_english: Update the underwriting COGS rate for customer group C1 in period 85 to 25% in input_cogs, then run the optimizer.
